title: Allantodia wheeleri
intro: Allantodia wheeleri, also known as Wheeler's fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Allantodia wheeleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Allantodia wheeleri is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a decoction, or dried and used as a powder in capsules or tablets.
taxonomy: Allantodia wheeleri is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This genus, Allantodia, belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. As a member of the Athyriaceae family, Allantodia wheeleri is closely related to other fern species within this family. The classification of Allantodia wheeleri reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants. The exact classification of Allantodia wheeleri may be subject to change as new research and discoveries are made.
